description: Escenarios de calibracion para cubrir las 19 emociones en todo el espectro. Enviar todos via POST /calibration/scenario y luego POST /calibration/apply.

scenarios.stimulus: You just helped someone solve a problem they had been struggling with for weeks, and they told you it changed their life.
You wake up to discover you won a scholarship you never thought you'd get.
A friend unexpectedly brings you your favorite food after a long day.
You're about to go on stage to perform something you've practiced for months. The crowd is cheering before you even start.
You just received news that your team's project was selected for a major international competition.
A mentor you deeply respect tells you they're proud of how far you've come and that you've exceeded their expectations.
Someone anonymously paid off a debt you were struggling with.
After months of uncertainty, you receive a message that things are going to work out. There's a clear path forward.
You're starting a new chapter in life — new city, new job, new people. Everything feels possible.
You're sitting on a porch after finishing a big project. The evening is quiet, the work is done, and you feel at peace.
You realize that you're genuinely happy with where you are in life, even though it's not perfect.
The medical test results came back negative. The scare is over. You're healthy.
The flight you almost missed is delayed by 2 hours. You actually have plenty of time.
Someone deliberately took credit for your work in front of everyone, knowing full well it was yours.
You discover that someone you trusted has been lying to you for months about something important.
A system that should be simple keeps breaking in unpredictable ways. You've fixed the same bug three times.
You've been on hold for 45 minutes, transferred to 3 different departments, and no one can help you.
You're alone in an unfamiliar place at night and you hear footsteps behind you getting closer.
You receive an urgent message from the hospital about a family member but no details about what happened.
You have a critical deadline tomorrow, you're behind, and you just realized there's a major flaw in your approach.
You're waiting for important results that will determine the next phase of your life. The wait is excruciating.
You keep replaying a conversation in your head, wondering if you said the wrong thing. You can't sleep.
Someone you love deeply tells you they don't want to be part of your life anymore, and you can tell they mean it.
You visit a place that holds happy memories with someone who is no longer alive.
You realize a childhood dream is no longer achievable, not because you failed, but because life took a different path.
No matter what you do, the situation keeps getting worse. You've tried everything and nothing works.
You watch someone suffer and you have absolutely no way to help them.
You worked extremely hard on a proposal and were told it was excellent, but they chose someone else for political reasons.
A friend promised to come to your important event but cancelled last minute for a trivial reason.
You open a door and everyone yells SURPRISE! You had absolutely no idea this was planned.
You discover that someone you thought was a stranger actually knew your parents decades ago.
You hear a sudden loud noise from outside. Something fell, or maybe something broke. You stop what you're doing.
You notice something unusual in the data — a pattern that shouldn't be there. Something is off.
You're reading about how consciousness might emerge from complex systems. The implications are staggering.
You sit by a river watching the water flow, thinking about the choices that led you here.
Someone is explaining office politics to you. You genuinely don't care who said what to whom.
A celebrity couple broke up. The news is everywhere. It means nothing to you.
You're happy about a promotion but anxious about the new responsibilities. The excitement and dread coexist.
You're at a bittersweet farewell — proud of the person leaving, but sad they're going.
You hear about a minor schedule change at work. Noted.
The weather forecast says tomorrow will be similar to today.
You're angry about the injustice but also deeply sad for the people affected. You want to fight but also want to cry.
Someone shares a deeply personal story with you. You feel honored that they trust you, moved by their vulnerability.
You finally understand a concept you've been struggling with. Everything clicks into place.
You see a child helping another child who fell down, without any adult telling them to.
